text: Autoregressive decoding in Transformer models is frequently bottlenecked by the memory bandwidth required to load key-value caches. While multi-query attention (MQA) alleviates this overhead by using a single key-value head, it often results in reduced model quality and necessitates costly training from scratch. In this paper, we present two primary contributions to improve inference efficiency. First, we introduce a method for "uptraining" existing multi-head attention (MHA) checkpoints into MQA models, achieving high performance with only 5% of the original pre-training compute. Second, we propose grouped-query attention (GQA), a generalized approach that uses an intermediate number of key-value heads. Our results demonstrate that GQA effectively interpolates between MHA and MQA, delivering model quality close to MHA while maintaining inference speeds comparable to MQA. These methods provide a resource-efficient path toward deploying high-capacity LLMs with significantly reduced latency.
